data.title: Brazil's Central Bank Considers Economic Impact of Iran Conflict
data.description: Brazil's central bank is closely monitoring the economic repercussions of the ongoing conflict in Iran as it prepares to initiate an easing cycle later this month. Bloomberg posted on X that a key policymaker from the bank emphasized the importance of considering the potential fallout from the situation in Iran. The central bank is assessing how the conflict might influence global economic conditions and, consequently, Brazil's economic landscape. This evaluation is crucial as the bank plans its monetary policy adjustments. The central bank's approach reflects a cautious stance in response to international developments that could affect Brazil's economy.

data.title: Iran Denies Involvement in Nakhchivan Explosion
data.description: Iran's Foreign Minister, Araghchi, held a phone conversation with Azerbaijan's Foreign Minister regarding an explosion in the Nakhchivan Autonomous Republic. According to Jin10, Iran has denied firing any shells into the region. The armed forces are currently investigating the incident.

data.title: Hormuz Shipping Disruptions and Smelter Shutdowns Pose Risks to Aluminum Supply
data.description:
Recent disruptions in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and shutdowns at key smelting facilities are raising concerns about the global aluminum supply. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the potential impact of these developments on the industry.

The Strait of Hormuz, a vital maritime passage for global trade, has experienced increased tensions, affecting the transportation of raw materials essential for aluminum production. Concurrently, several smelters have ceased operations due to various challenges, including energy shortages and geopolitical tensions.

These factors are contributing to uncertainty in the aluminum market, with potential implications for prices and availability. Industry experts are closely monitoring the situation, as prolonged disruptions could exacerbate supply chain issues and impact manufacturers reliant on aluminum.

The aluminum industry is particularly sensitive to geopolitical and logistical challenges, given its reliance on specific raw materials and energy-intensive production processes. As the situation unfolds, stakeholders are urged to consider contingency plans to mitigate potential supply disruptions.

While the full extent of the impact remains to be seen, the current developments underscore the importance of stable shipping routes and operational smelters in maintaining a steady aluminum supply.

data.title: Tokenization and Internet Capital Markets: Democratizing Global Investments
data.description:
Blockchain tokenization and 'Internet Capital Markets' are poised to democratize access to global investments, according to recent findings. By the end of February, tokenized real-world assets achieved a market capitalization of $23.4 billion. According to NS3.AI, CoinDesk Research indicates that the stablecoin market capitalization is nearing an all-time high of $310 billion, with stablecoins accounting for 13.3% of the digital asset market in February.

The article further explores how advisors can leverage regulated vehicles, Solana-based infrastructure, and cost-effective on-chain transactions to construct fractional, global portfolios accessible to a broader client base. This approach aims to enhance investment opportunities and diversify portfolios across different asset classes, making them available to a wider audience.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Expresses Support for Netanyahu Amid Iran Tensions
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has stated his desire for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to remain undisturbed, except in matters concerning conflict with Iran. According to Jin10, Trump emphasized the importance of Netanyahu's leadership in navigating the complexities of the Middle East, particularly in relation to Iran's activities. The statement underscores the ongoing geopolitical tensions in the region and the strategic alliance between the United States and Israel. Trump's remarks reflect his administration's stance on prioritizing stability and security in the Middle East, with a focus on countering Iranian influence.

data.title: BNB Drops Below 650 USDT with a 1.51% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 05, 2026, 16:06 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 650 USDT and is now trading at 649.950012 USDT, with a narrowed 1.51% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Nearly $50 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network in the past hour, with $27.33 million in BTC liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $49.82 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including $43.65 million in long positions and $6.16 million in short positions. In addition, $27.33 million in BTC positions were liquidated and $14.26 million in ETH positions were liquidated.

data.title: Carlyle Group's Future in Private Equity Discussed
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting a discussion about the future of private equity, specifically focusing on Carlyle Group. The commentary suggests that private equity is not facing permanent challenges and that Carlyle is strategically positioned to navigate current market conditions. The analysis indicates optimism about Carlyle's ability to adapt and succeed despite existing hurdles in the private equity sector.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump and Congress Show Interest in Public Opinion
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. U.S. President Donald Trump and members of Congress appear to be paying close attention to public sentiment, according to recent discussions. This observation highlights the administration's focus on aligning with the views and concerns of the populace. The engagement of political leaders with public opinion is seen as a crucial aspect of governance, reflecting a commitment to addressing the needs and expectations of citizens.

data.title: Aave Labs Proposes Bug Bounty Program for Aave V4
data.description:
Aave Labs has proposed the launch of a bug bounty program specifically for Aave V4. According to Foresight News, this initiative will be hosted on the security platform Sherlock. The program aims to create a secure reporting channel for Aave V4, featuring a tiered processing mechanism to minimize spam reports, prioritize high-risk vulnerabilities, and expedite the handling process.

Sherlock has previously collaborated with Aave contributors on early security efforts for both Aave V3 and V4, establishing shared standards for reporting, classification, and escalation.

data.title: EU to Explore Short-Term Measures for Energy Price Reduction Amid Green Transition
data.description: The European Union is preparing to evaluate short-term strategies aimed at reducing energy prices in the upcoming years. Bloomberg posted on X that these discussions are part of the broader efforts to facilitate the transition to greener energy sources. As the EU moves towards sustainable energy solutions, the focus remains on balancing immediate economic needs with long-term environmental goals. The initiative underscores the EU's commitment to addressing energy costs while advancing its green agenda.

data.title: Polygon Introduces On-Chain AI Agent Toolkit
data.description: Polygon has unveiled a new on-chain AI agent toolkit named Polygon Agent CLI, aimed at facilitating AI agents in their creation and operation on the Polygon blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this development marks a significant step in integrating artificial intelligence with blockchain technology, potentially enhancing the efficiency and capabilities of AI-driven applications within the Polygon ecosystem.

data.title: IRS Proposes New Rules for Digital Asset Tax Reporting
data.description:
The U.S. Internal Revenue Service (IRS) has proposed new regulations allowing cryptocurrency exchanges to require customers to receive tax forms electronically, such as the 1099-DA form for reporting total gains from digital asset transactions. According to ChainCatcher, exchanges previously had to offer paper form options.

Under the new tax reporting system implemented this year, cryptocurrency exchanges must report total transaction gains and cost basis, enabling the IRS to automatically obtain detailed profit and loss data. This move aims to enhance compliance oversight of digital asset holders. The proposed regulations also permit exchanges to terminate business relationships with customers who refuse electronic receipt of tax forms.

The proposal is not yet finalized and is currently open for public comment. Reports from crypto tax software platforms indicate a significant increase in IRS warning letters to U.S. users, reminding them that cryptocurrency transactions may be taxable and must be reported according to regulations.

data.title: M&A Environment Highlights Lazard's Role in Investment Strategies
data.description: John W. Rogers Jr. of Ariel Investments emphasizes the significance of firms like Lazard in the current mergers and acquisitions landscape. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Rogers' perspective on the importance of maintaining key holdings in today's market environment. Rogers suggests that Lazard's strategic position makes it a valuable asset for investors navigating the complexities of M&A activities.

data.title: U.S. House Committee Seeks Answers from Travel CEOs on AI Pricing Strategies
data.description: The U.S. House Oversight Committee has reached out to the CEOs of major travel companies, including Uber, Lyft, and Expedia, requesting information on their use of artificial intelligence and consumer data in pricing strategies. According to Jin10, the committee is investigating how these companies leverage AI technologies to set prices and the potential implications for consumers. The inquiry aims to understand the balance between technological advancements and consumer protection in the travel industry. The committee's actions reflect growing concerns about the transparency and fairness of AI-driven pricing models.

data.title: The IRS plans to allow cryptocurrency exchanges like Coinbase to submit tax forms electronically.
data.description: The U.S. Internal Revenue Service (IRS) proposed new rules on Thursday to allow cryptocurrency exchanges such as Coinbase and Kraken to submit tax forms electronically, such as the 1099-DA form reporting total revenue from digital asset trading, whereas previously exchanges were required to offer paper forms. Under the new tax filing system implemented this year, cryptocurrency exchanges are required to report total revenue and cost basis. The IRS will automatically obtain detailed profit and loss data, thereby strengthening compliance oversight of cryptocurrency holders. The new rules also allow exchanges to terminate business relationships with customers who refuse to receive tax forms electronically. The proposal has not yet been finalized and is currently open for public comment. It is understood that cryptocurrency tax software platforms such as CoinLedger have reported a significant increase in the number of IRS warning letters received by U.S. users, reminding them that cryptocurrency transactions may be subject to taxation and required reporting. (The Block)

data.title: SEC Seeks Public Input on Capital-Raising Policies for Small Businesses
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is inviting public input on ways to enhance capital-raising policies for small businesses. The SEC posted on X, urging individuals to submit their recommendations before the annual Small Business Forum scheduled for March 9. The selected priorities will be presented to Congress. Submissions are due today at noon.

data.title: Ripple Expands Payments Platform with Managed Custody and Virtual-Account Collections
data.description: Ripple has enhanced its payments platform by introducing managed custody and virtual-account collections, aiming to provide an "end-to-end" service. According to NS3.AI, this platform has processed over $100 billion across more than 60 markets. Additionally, Ripple's prime broker, Hidden Road Partners CIV US LLC, has been included in the NSCC MPID directory for its inaugural OTC trade, integrating a Ripple entity into a key U.S. stock-clearing system. These developments align with Ripple's strategy to connect traditional finance, stablecoins, and the XRP Ledger, while maintaining optional usage of XRP.
